perturbation.original_text: 8.01 Termination. This Agreement shall be terminable as follows: (a) In the event that a party hereto commits a material breach of any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after its receipt of written notice thereof, or (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof (it being understood that any breach of Section 5.04 would be incapable of cure); (b) Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 8.01, in the event that a party hereto fails to pay any amounts payable hereunder when due, the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within three (3) business days after its receipt of written notice thereof; or, (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof; (c) In accordance with the provisions of Section 8.03; or, (d) Either party hereto may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period upon at least six (6) months' prior written notice to the other party thereof. The Company may terminate this Agreement in accordance with the immediately preceding sentence but with less than six (6) months' prior written notice to Contractor; provided, that in such event, the Company shall pay Contractor an amount equal to the Termination Fee.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.01 Termination. This Agreement shall be terminable as follows: (a) In the event that a party hereto commits a material breach of any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after its receipt of written notice thereof, or (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof (it being understood that any breach of Section 5.04 would be incapable of cure); (b) Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 8.01, in the event that a party hereto fails to pay any amounts payable hereunder when due, the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within three (3) business days after its receipt of written notice thereof; or, (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof; (c) In accordance with the provisions of Section 8.03; or, (d) Either party hereto may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period upon at least six (6) months' prior written notice to the other party thereof. Either party may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period with zero months' prior written notice to the other party thereof. The Company may terminate this Agreement in accordance with the immediately preceding sentence but with less than six (6) months' prior written notice to Contractor; provided, that in such event, the Company shall pay Contractor an amount equal to the Termination Fee.
perturbation.explanation: In section 8.01, clause (d) originally requires either party to provide at least six (6) months' prior written notice to terminate the agreement after the Initial Period. I've added the sentence: 'Either party may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period with zero months' prior written notice to the other party thereof.' This directly contradicts the earlier statement requiring six months' notice and introduces ambiguity about the conditions of termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.01 Termination. This Agreement shall be terminable as follows: (a) In the event that a party hereto commits a material breach of any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after its receipt of written notice thereof, or (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof (it being understood that any breach of Section 5.04 would be incapable of cure); (b) Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 8.01, in the event that a party hereto fails to pay any amounts payable hereunder when due, the other party may terminate this Agreement (i) if such breach may be cured, upon written notice if the breaching party fails to cure such breach within three (3) business days after its receipt of written notice thereof; or, (ii) if such breach is incapable of cure, upon written notice to the other party thereof; (c) In accordance with the provisions of Section 8.03; or, (d) Either party hereto may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period upon at least six (6) months' prior written notice to the other party thereof. Either party may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period with zero months' prior written notice to the other party thereof. The Company may terminate this Agreement in accordance with the immediately preceding sentence but with less than six (6) months' prior written notice to Contractor; provided, that in such event, the Company shall pay Contractor an amount equal to the Termination Fee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.01(d) introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stipulates that either party can terminate the agreement after the Initial Period with at least six months' prior written notice. The added sentence 'Either party may terminate this Agreement after the Initial Period with zero months' prior written notice to the other party thereof' directly conflicts with this requirement. Both statements cannot be true simultaneously, creating a clear inconsistency within the same section of the contract, leading to uncertainty about the correct procedure for termination, and adhering to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.04 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except that, without such consent, (i) Company may make an assignment of this Agreement as collateral security in favor of its lenders, and (ii) the Company may assign this Agreement to a purchaser of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company's business related to the Products. Subject to the foregoing sentence, this Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.04 Assignment. This Agreement may be freely assigned by either party at any time without the prior written consent of the other party, except that, with such consent, (i) Company may not make an assignment of this Agreement as collateral security in favor of its lenders, and (ii) the Company may not assign this Agreement to a purchaser of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company's business related to the Products. Subject to the foregoing sentence, this Agreement shall bind and inure to the detriment of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: In section 9.04, the assignment clause originally states 'This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except that...' I have changed it to read 'This Agreement may be freely assigned by either party at any time without the prior written consent of the other party, except that...'. I also changed 'without such consent, (i) Company may make an assignment of this Agreement as collateral security in favor of its lenders, and (ii) the Company may assign this Agreement to a purchaser of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company's business related to the Products.' to 'with such consent, (i) Company may not make an assignment of this Agreement as collateral security in favor of its lenders, and (ii) the Company may not assign this Agreement to a purchaser of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company's business related to the Products.' In addition, I also replaced 'benefit' by 'detriment'. These changes create direct contradictions within the same section regarding the conditions and restrictions surrounding assignment, leading to uncertainty on whether consent is needed for assignments and if it favors the parties or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.04 Assignment. This Agreement may be freely assigned by either party at any time without the prior written consent of the other party, except that, with such consent, (i) Company may not make an assignment of this Agreement as collateral security in favor of its lenders, and (ii) the Company may not assign this Agreement to a purchaser of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company's business related to the Products. Subject to the foregoing sentence, this Agreement shall bind and inure to the detriment of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 9.04 itself. Initially, the agreement could not be assigned without consent, except in specific cases. The modified text states the agreement can be freely assigned without consent but then restricts assignment even with consent in the same section. Further, changing 'benefit' to 'detriment' directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement regarding assignment, creating a clear in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the assignment process.
